title: S.A.M Layered Patch
description: An additive patch that modifies an existing data file (a vanilla file like 'items/items.json', or another mod's file) WITHOUT replacing it. S.A.M reads the currently-resolved target, applies every mod's operations in load order (stacking, not overwriting), and writes the merged result to a private overlay it mounts at high priority. If two mods edit the same field, last-loaded wins and S.A.M logs a warning. A target that doesn't exist is logged and skipped — never a crash.

$schema.description: Optional. URL of this JSON Schema, so editors give autocomplete + validation as you type. Ignored by S.A.M itself.

target.description: Logical path of the data file to patch, as Barony reads it, e.g. 'items/items.json' or 'data/gameplaymodifiers.json'. Must be a file Barony loads via its data-file system.

operations.description: Operations applied in order to the target's JSON. Later operations see the result of earlier ones (and of earlier-loaded mods).

op.description: edit_field: set an EXISTING field to 'value' (never replaces a whole entry; a missing path is warned + skipped, catching typos). add_entry: set/create the field at 'path' to 'value' (adds a new key). remove_field: delete the field at 'path'. multiply_field: multiply the numeric field at 'path' by the numeric 'value' (integer fields stay integers) — handy for balance mods.

path.description: Dot-separated path into the target JSON, matching the file's real structure. For items/items.json the item keys are LOWERCASE internal names and fields use snake_case, e.g. 'items.iron_sword.gold_value' or 'items.wooden_shield.weight_value'. (Keys are looked up literally; use the names exactly as they appear in the file.)

value.description: The new value (edit_field/add_entry) or the numeric factor (multiply_field). Omitted for remove_field. May be any JSON type for edit_field/add_entry; must be a number for multiply_field.
